channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ruto warns against court challenges to affordable housing initiative
item.description: President William Ruto has strongly spoken out against individuals or groups threatening to drag the government's affordable housing programme to court. He said that such moves are not only counterpro...

item.title: European Nations and Japan Commit to Ensuring Safe Shipping in Strait of Hormuz Amid Tensions
item.description: Leaders from the United Kingdom, France, Germany, Italy, the Netherlands, and Japan have collectively expressed their readiness to support international efforts to ensure the safe passage of commercia...

item.title: Suspected Filipino spy reveals method of obtaining sensitive Coast Guard information for alleged Chinese network
item.description: Isang Pilipino na hinihinalang sangkot sa isang espionage network na may koneksyon umano sa China ang nagbahagi ng detalye kung paano siya nakakuha at nakapagpasa ng sensitibong impormasyon mula sa Ph...

item.title: Alex Eala wins three-set match over Laura Siegemund to reach next round at Miami Open
item.description: Filipino tennis rising star Alex Eala delivered an impressive performance at the Miami Open, coming from behind to defeat Germany’s Laura Siegemund in a tough three-set match. The encounter showcased ...

item.title: Judge to Rule on Sentence for Brandon Tobin in Manslaughter of Grandmother After Drug-Related Seizure
item.description: In St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, Brandon Tobin faces sentencing for the 2022 death of his grandmother, Mildred Brake, following a cocaine-induced seizure that triggered violent behaviour. Tob...

item.title: Vice President Nchimbi Launches National Tree Planting Campaign in Lindi
item.description: LINDI: Tanzania's Vice President, Emmanuel Nchimbi, officially launched the International Day of Forests and the National Tree Planting Campaign 2026 at Ilulu Stadium in Lindi Region. He warned that t...

item.title: Kyle Sandilands controversy highlights ongoing concerns about media regulation and culture in Sydney
item.description: The article argues that the apparent end of Kyle Sandilands’ contract and the “Kyle and Jackie O” radio show is not an isolated incident, but rather another example of long-standing issues within Aust...

item.title: Police file murder complaint in killing of Miss Earth 2013 runner-up Lullete Jane Ramilo
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) announced progress in its investigation into the killing of Lullete Jane Ramilo-De Guzman, a former Miss Philippines Earth 2013 runner-up who was fatally shot in S...

item.title: Bhabanipur Voter Sentiments Ahead of West Bengal 2026 Elections
item.description: As West Bengal gears up for its 2026 elections, Bhabanipur, the high-profile constituency, is witnessing a close political contest. On one hand, supporters of the incumbent, popularly known as Bengal'...

item.title: Government Allocates Sh3.5 Billion to Boost Tea Farming and Factory Operations
item.description: Kenya's government has taken a big step to support tea farmers by injecting Sh3.5 billion into the tea industry. The funding is aimed at upgrading factories to meet international standards and promote...

item.title: ABC cancels Taylor Frankie Paul’s Bachelorette season following resurfaced assault video
item.description: Disney Entertainment Television, through its ABC network, has cancelled the anticipated season of 'The Bachelorette' featuring influencer Taylor Frankie Paul just days before the scheduled premiere. T...

item.title: Zanele Muholi’s Visual Activism Highlights Black LGBTIQ+ Lives Globally
item.description: Zanele Muholi, a queer South African visual activist, has spent over two decades using photography to document and celebrate the lives of Black lesbians and trans people. Preferring the term 'visual a...

item.title: Jemima alleges govt is delaying visas for Imran Khan’s sons, urges proper access
item.description: Jemima Goldsmith, ex-wife of former Prime Minister Imran Khan, has publicly accused the Pakistani government of deliberately delaying visas for her sons, Kasim and Sulaiman, who live in London. She cl...

item.title: Gauteng High Court confirms widower's right to remain in retirement home
item.description: The Gauteng High Court in Johannesburg has upheld the rights of a 78-year-old widower to continue living in his late wife's retirement unit, despite his name not being on the original life-rights agre...

item.title: UCT research reveals high rates of depression and anxiety among Western Cape schoolchildren
item.description: A new study by the University of Cape Town (UCT) has found that nearly one in three learners in the Western Cape aged between 10 and 14 display symptoms of depression, while one in five experience sig...

item.title: President Ruto Backs Affordable Housing Programme at End of Western Kenya Development Tour
item.description: President William Ruto wrapped up his tour of Western Kenya by firmly defending his Affordable Housing Programme. He called it a vital initiative, especially in areas with high population and land sho...

item.title: Delhi High Court Orders Removal of Defamatory Content Against Isha Foundation and Sadhguru
item.description: On 19th March 2026, the Delhi High Court issued an interim order directing the Tamil media outlet, Nakkheeran Media, to remove videos and articles deemed defamatory against the Isha Foundation and its...

item.title: Simba and Azam face vital away fixtures as Mainland League title race heats up
item.description: The NBC Premier League resumes today with two of Tanzania’s top teams, Simba SC and Azam FC, facing crucial away matches that could heavily influence the title race. Simba travel to Mwanza to face Pam...

item.title: FCC Official Involved in Potential Overreach Targeting Disney
item.description: Records have revealed that an official within the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) allegedly offered assistance to Brendan Carr, a Republican commissioner, in targeting Disney. The documents in...

item.title: Supreme Court to Review Legal Challenges to Canada’s Assault-Style Firearm Ban
item.description: The Supreme Court of Canada has agreed to hear four appeals challenging the federal Liberal government's ban on over 2,500 so-called 'assault-style' firearms. The ban, initially introduced in May 2020...
